sourceTitle: Database properties help organize your team’s information

questions.question: Which property type lets you assign only one tag or value to a page?

options.text: Select
options.explanation: Select properties allow you to choose a single option from a predefined list, perfect for single‑value tagging.

options.text: Multi-select
options.explanation: Multi-select lets you choose multiple tags, not just one.

options.text: Person
options.explanation: Person tags a user, but it’s not limited to a single predefined label like Select.

options.text: Date
options.explanation: Date stores calendar information, not tags.

questions.question: If you need to assign several attributes (e.g., multiple skills) to a single page, which property should you use?

options.text: Select
options.explanation: Select only permits one choice per entry.

options.text: Multi-select
options.explanation: Multi-select lets you pick multiple values, ideal for listing several skills.

options.text: Date
options.explanation: Date records a point in time, not categorical attributes.

options.text: Checkbox
options.explanation: Checkbox is a binary true/false field, not for multiple selections.

questions.question: Which property automatically records the exact moment a page is first created?

options.text: Created time
options.explanation: Created time is a system‑generated timestamp that marks when a page is first made.

options.text: Last edited time
options.explanation: Last edited time updates whenever the page changes, not when it’s created.

options.text: Formula
options.explanation: Formula calculates values based on other properties; it doesn’t store creation timestamps.

options.text: Rollup
options.explanation: Rollup aggregates data from related items; it doesn’t capture creation time.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a Relation property?

options.text: To hide a property from certain views
options.explanation: Hiding properties is done via the visibility settings, not through Relations.

options.text: To connect two separate databases so their records can reference each other
options.explanation: Relations create a link between items in different databases, enabling cross‑database references.

options.text: To automatically calculate sums or averages
options.explanation: Calculations are performed by Formula or Rollup properties, not Relations.

options.text: To store a single date range for a project
options.explanation: Date ranges belong to the Date property type.

questions.question: You want a view that only shows the project’s DRI and launch date. How can you achieve this?

options.text: Delete all other properties from the database
options.explanation: Deleting properties removes data permanently; you can instead hide them in the view.

options.text: Toggle the visibility of properties using the Properties menu at the top of the database
options.explanation: You can turn specific properties on or off for a given view, showing only what you need.

options.text: Change the property type of other columns to Text
options.explanation: Changing types doesn’t affect visibility; it only alters the data format.

options.text: Create a Formula that concatenates all other properties into one column
options.explanation: Formulas combine data but don’t hide other columns.

questions.question: What distinguishes a Select property from a Multi-select property?

options.text: Select allows only one option per entry, while Multi-select permits multiple options.
options.explanation: Select is for single‑choice tagging; Multi-select lets you assign several tags to the same record.

options.text: Select stores dates, while Multi-select stores text.
options.explanation: Both store categorical data, not dates.

options.text: Select can be hidden, but Multi-select cannot.
options.explanation: Visibility is independent of the property type.

options.text: Select automatically creates a related database, while Multi-select does not.
options.explanation: Relations are a separate property type; Select and Multi-select are only tagging tools.

questions.question: Which property type would you choose to store a candidate’s LinkedIn profile link?

options.text: URL
options.explanation: The URL property is designed for web addresses such as LinkedIn profiles.

options.text: Email
options.explanation: Email stores email addresses, not generic web links.

options.text: Phone
options.explanation: Phone stores telephone numbers.

options.text: Text
options.explanation: While you could paste a link into a Text property, the URL type provides link‑specific functionality.

questions.question: How does a Rollup property work together with a Relation property?

options.text: Rollup aggregates or calculates data from the items that are linked via the Relation.
options.explanation: Rollups pull in values from related records (established by a Relation) and can sum, count, average, etc.

options.text: Rollup creates a new database that mirrors the related one.
options.explanation: Rollup does not duplicate databases; it only summarizes data.

options.text: Rollup hides the Relation column from the view.
options.explanation: Visibility is controlled separately; Rollup doesn’t hide other columns.

options.text: Rollup changes the data type of the Relation to a Date.
options.explanation: Rollup doesn’t alter the underlying Relation’s type; it merely computes based on it.

questions.question: When would a Checkbox property be more appropriate than a Formula property?

options.text: When you need a simple true/false indicator to track task completion.
options.explanation: Checkboxes provide a binary on/off toggle, perfect for marking steps as done.

options.text: When you need to calculate the total cost of a project.
options.explanation: Cost calculations require arithmetic, which is handled by Formula.

options.text: When you want to store a URL for a resource.
options.explanation: URLs belong in a URL property, not a Checkbox.

options.text: When you need to connect two databases together.
options.explanation: Relations are used for linking databases, not Checkbox.

questions.question: Which property type is best suited for assigning a project manager to a task?

options.text: Person
options.explanation: Person properties let you select a teammate, making them ideal for assigning owners.

options.text: Select
options.explanation: Select can tag a name, but it doesn’t link to a user profile like Person does.

options.text: Multi-select
options.explanation: Multi-select allows multiple tags, which isn’t necessary for a single manager.

options.text: Date
options.explanation: Date records time information, not people.
